title: salix phylicifolia subsp. raetica
intro: Salix phylicifolia subsp. raetica is a subspecies of willow tree, belonging to the Salicaceae family, native to the European Alps. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia subsp. raetica include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating fever, as well as its use in herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and salves. Its herbal preparations are often used to alleviate menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other conditions related to inflammation and pain.
